name: EB28 Desk OS
audience: Indie hackers, retail algo-traders, prediction-market and Robinhood power users, build-in-public founders.
voice: Sharp indie-hacker/quant, anti-guru, direct, technical-but-accessible, build-in-public. No hype, no emoji spam.
positioning: A local-first fleet of autonomous trading agents behind a kill switch. The safety system is the product; radical transparency is the proof.
proofPoints: The live tape is public and includes real losses — currently down ~$61, printed on purpose.
Founder ran 9 live bots and shut the whole floor down with one command. That kill switch is the product.
Gated runner, capital guard, per-desk circuit breakers, trade journal, live dashboard.
Runs local-first on your own Mac — your keys, your kill switch.
Works with Polymarket, Kalshi (Simmer SDK) and Robinhood's official Agentic Trading MCP.
8 agents, one obsession each. License one for $47, the full fleet + OS for $197, operator install $497.
rules: NO income claims. NO passive-income / get-rich / money-printer / guaranteed-returns language.
NO performance promises. It is a software license, not investment advice.
Always imply real risk of loss.
Lead with the safety system and transparency, never returns.
Buy-from-people-who-show-you-the-tape is the core trust line.
ctas: Watch it trade live (losses included): eb28.co/fundmanager
riskLine: Software license, not investment advice. Trading carries real risk of loss — including ours.
